title: Tinus orinocensis
intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Tinus orinocensis. However, I found information on the plant "Tinospora" also known as "Tinospora cordifolia" but also "Tinospora orinocensis" is a synonym for  "Tinospora crux-malayana". 

Tinospora crux-malayana, also known as "Tinospora" or "Guduchi", is a plant species used in traditional medicine. It has been reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Tinospora crux-malayana include alkaloids, glycosides, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tinospora crux-malayana include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems.
taxonomy: Tinus orinocensis is classified within the plant family Rutaceae, which includes other citrus and rue species. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and genetic characteristics. The Rutaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, including Aurantioideae and Rutoideae. Tinus orinocensis is a member of the Aurantioideae subfamily, which is primarily composed of citrus and related species. The exact classification of Tinus orinocensis may be subject to ongoing research and revisions.
